COMMENT_TS: Credit transfer data were partly available in 2012, since consumer payments and banking payments were performed by a single Electronic Fund Transfer System (EFT) in Turkey until December 2012. As of this date EFT has been separated into a retail payment system for consumer payments and a large value payment system for banking payments.  In this context, credit transfer data include yearly retail payments data in EFT system since 2013, while credit transfer data of 2012 include only retail payments in EFT system within December 2012.

COMMENT_TS: Total figure does not include payments by "card with a delayed debit function" or "other payment instruments". Breakdown figures for "credit transfers", "direct debits" and "cheques" include estimates of on-us payments. Breakdown figures for "cheques", "card and e-money payments", exlucluding the MEMO items, and "cash withdrawals", derive from the triennial Federal Reserve Payment Study, which began in 2000 and estimates annual figures every three years; figures for other years are derived from those estimates.  The 2015-2023 figures for credit transfers and direct debits have been revised for consistency with recent estimates from the Federal Reserve Payments Study.

TITLE: Türkiye - Volume of cashless payments, credit transfers
COMMENT_TS: Credit transfer data were partly available in 2012, since consumer payments and banking payments were performed by a single Electronic Fund Transfer System (EFT) in Turkey until December 2012. As of this date EFT has been separated into a retail payment system for consumer payments and a large value payment system for banking payments.  In this context, credit transfer data include yearly retail payments data in EFT system since 2013, while credit transfer data of 2012 include only retail payments in EFT system within December 2012.

COMMENT_TS: Total figure does not include payments by "card with a delayed debit function" or "other payment instruments". Breakdown figures for "credit transfers", "direct debits" and "cheques" include estimates of on-us payments. Breakdown figures for "cheques", "card and e-money payments", exlucluding the MEMO items, and "cash withdrawals", derive from the triennial Federal Reserve Payment Study, which began in 2000 and estimates annual figures every three years; figures for other years are derived from those estimates.  The 2015-2023 figures for credit transfers and direct debits have been revised for consistency with recent estimates from the Federal Reserve Payments Study.

The 2015-2023 figures for credit transfers and direct debits have been revised for consistency with recent estimates from the Federal Reserve Payments Study.
COMMENT_OBS: Revised
